headlines.headline: Russia suspected of using Swedish church near airport for espionage activities
headlines.summary: Swedish intelligence services suspect Moscow is using a Russian Orthodox church near Vasteras airport as a platform for espionage and hybrid activities, warning it could threaten national security.

headlines.headline: Only 25% of F-35 fleet fully mission capable as readiness rates decline
headlines.summary: A GAO report reveals the F-35's full mission capable rate fell to 25% through fiscal 2025, with readiness rates continuing to decline amid ongoing maintenance and sustainment challenges.

headlines.headline: US plans major reduction of jets and warships for NATO operations in Europe
headlines.summary: The United States plans to significantly reduce the aircraft and warships it makes available for NATO operations in Europe, according to a New York Times report.

headlines.headline: Russia building major troop deployment infrastructure along NATO's Northern Flank
headlines.summary: Russia is constructing facilities for tens of thousands of troops near the Arctic, while NATO bolsters defenses with a new Arctic combat force as tensions rise on the alliance's northern border.

headlines.headline: Germany and Spain launch 'Team Gen 6' after FCAS fighter program collapses
headlines.summary: Germany and Spain are forming a new coalition to develop sixth-generation fighter technology after the Franco-German FCAS program collapsed over irreconcilable differences on requirements.

headlines.headline: Twelve Killed in Missouri Skydiving Plane Crash
headlines.summary: A plane carrying a pilot and 11 passengers on a skydiving outing crashed near Butler Memorial Airport in Missouri, killing all aboard. The aircraft caught fire shortly after takeoff around 11:30 AM local time.

headlines.headline: G7 Summit Begins in France as Anti-G7 Protests Erupt in Geneva
headlines.summary: World leaders convened for the G7 summit in France as thousands of protesters clashed with police in Geneva. Swiss authorities deployed water cannons and tear gas to disperse demonstrators ahead of the meeting.

headlines.headline: Kyiv's historic Pechersk Lavra monastery burns as Russian missiles strike capital
headlines.summary: One of Ukraine's most sacred Orthodox sites caught fire during a Russian ballistic missile attack on Kyiv, with church leaders calling it "a crime against humanity, against history, against Christianity."

headlines.headline: Armenia releases final election results as pro-Russian opposition demands new vote
headlines.summary: Prime Minister Pashinyan's Civil Contract party won 64 of 105 parliamentary seats, but pro-Russian opposition groups are contesting the results and demanding new elections amid tensions over Armenia's pivot away from Moscow.

headlines.headline: Russian Black Sea Fleet command plans relocation from Crimea to Novorossiysk
headlines.summary: Pro-Ukrainian partisans claim Russian naval command is preparing to relocate its headquarters from occupied Crimea to the Russian mainland port of Novorossiysk, citing increasing Ukrainian strikes on the peninsula.

headlines.headline: Italian former general launches far-right party challenging Meloni government
headlines.summary: Roberto "Il Generale" Vannacci has founded a fast-rising far-right party that poses a threat to Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni's centre-right coalition and could reshape Italy's political landscape ahead of elections.

headlines.headline: Swiss voters reject proposal to cap population at 10 million
headlines.summary: Nearly 55% of Swiss voters rejected a right-wing initiative to limit the population by cutting migration, in a referendum that framed the issue around affordability and sustainability.

headlines.headline: Trump tells Putin he is prepared to help end war in Ukraine
headlines.summary: In a friendly and frank phone call, Trump also said the US is nearing a peace deal with Iran, according to a Putin adviser.

headlines.headline: US musician Oliver Tree among six killed in Brazil helicopter collision
headlines.summary: Two helicopters collided midair over Rio de Janeiro, killing all six people on board including the alternative singer and internet personality Oliver Tree, 32.

headlines.headline: China arrests US academic at conference for espionage activities
headlines.summary: Beijing authorities detained an American academic during a conference in China, accusing them of espionage, amid escalating US-China tensions.

perspectives.angle: China Arrests US Academic at Conference for Espionage
perspectives.summary: China detained Min Zin, a US academic specializing in Myanmar and Chinese foreign policy, during a conference on espionage charges. The arrest comes just a month after Trump visited Beijing amid already-strained US-China relations.

headlines.headline: British Royal Marines Seize Russian Shadow Fleet Oil Tanker
headlines.summary: UK forces intercepted an oil tanker in the English Channel suspected of being part of Russia sanctioned shadow fleet. Six-hour operation marks first time Britain acted alone to stop such a vessel.

perspectives.angle: British forces seize Russian shadow fleet oil tanker in Channel
perspectives.summary: Britain Defense Ministry intercepted an oil tanker believed to be part of Russia sanctioned shadow fleet, marking the first time British forces acted alone to stop a ship in the sanctions-evading fleet.

headlines.headline: US and Iran Reach Ceasefire Agreement to End War
headlines.summary: The deal halts fighting for 60 days, opens the Strait of Hormuz, and lifts the US naval blockade on Iranian ports. Pakistan mediated the agreement, with official signing scheduled in Geneva.

headlines.headline: Israel Expands Military Control by 1,000sq km Across Three Fronts
headlines.summary: Al Jazeera investigation reveals Israel has expanded military control by 1,000 square kilometers in Gaza, Lebanon, and Syria, using land grabs to mask strategic failures.

headlines.headline: UK and Japan Sign £18bn Investment Deal
headlines.summary: Japanese firms will invest billions in UK infrastructure and offshore wind projects. The agreement strengthens bilateral economic ties amid shifting global trade dynamics.

headlines.headline: Ukrainian Drone Strike Kills One at Russian Port of Temryuk
headlines.summary: A Ukrainian drone attack killed one person and sparked a fire at a sea terminal in the southern Russian port of Temryuk in the Krasnodar region, according to the regional governor.

perspectives.angle: Kharkiv Art Museum in flames after Russian drone strike injures infant
perspectives.summary: A major Ukrainian cultural site was set ablaze by a Russian drone attack that wounded five people including a one-month-old baby, in what officials called a "cynical" strike on heritage.

headlines.headline: US Military Strike Kills Leader of Venezuela's Tren de Aragua Gang
headlines.summary: President Trump announced that US forces carried out a strike killing Hector Rusthenford Guerrero Flores (Niño Guerrero), leader of the Venezuelan prison gang Tren de Aragua. The gang has expanded across Latin America and the US.

headlines.headline: Hezbollah Expects Lebanon to Be Included in US-Iran Peace Deal
headlines.summary: A senior Hezbollah politician expressed confidence that Iran will insist on including Lebanon in any agreement with the United States, as hopes grow for a potential peace deal between Tehran and Washington.

perspectives.angle: Hezbollah says Lebanon must be included in any US-Iran peace deal
perspectives.summary: A senior Hezbollah politician said the group is confident Iran will insist on Lebanon being part of any agreement with the United States, as hopes grow for a deal between Tehran and Washington.

headlines.headline: Kushner Albania Project Built on Disputed Land, Villagers Say
headlines.summary: Villagers in southern Albania claim a development project tied to Jared Kushner is being built on disputed land, with locals reporting being blocked from accessing their property by armed guards.

headlines.headline: China's New Loans Miss Forecasts as Property Slump Curbs Demand
headlines.summary: Chinese bank lending rose less than expected in May as the country's prolonged property downturn continued to weigh on household borrowing, missing analyst forecasts.

perspectives.angle: China Bank Lending Misses Forecasts as Property Slump Drags On
perspectives.summary: New bank lending in China rose less than expected in May after contracting the previous month, as a prolonged property downturn continued to weigh on household borrowing in the world's second-largest economy.

headlines.headline: Hezbollah confronts Israeli troops advancing in southern Lebanon amid ongoing strikes
headlines.summary: Hezbollah fighters fired rockets at Israeli troops near Majdal Zoun, forcing them to retreat, as Israel continued air strikes and issued evacuation warnings for 20 towns in southern Lebanon including Nabatieh.

headlines.headline: Ukrainian strike sets marine terminal ablaze in Russia Krasnodar Krai
headlines.summary: A Ukrainian strike hit the Tamanneftegaz liquefied petroleum gas terminal in Russia southern Krasnodar region, with monitoring groups reporting fire signatures at the facility.

headlines.headline: India successfully tests ballistic missile defence system with three flight trials
headlines.summary: India demonstrated its ballistic missile defence capability with three successful flight tests, showing its ability to intercept incoming long-range nuclear missiles and hostile aircraft.

headlines.headline: Croatia emerges as maritime gateway connecting Asia to Central Europe
headlines.summary: Croatia is strengthening its role as the maritime hub linking China and other Asian nations with Central and Eastern Europe, positioning itself as a key trade route amid shifting global supply chains.

headlines.headline: US Evangelicals Divided on Trump's Iran War and Immigration Crackdown, Poll Shows
headlines.summary: Evangelical Christians, who helped power Trump's 2024 election victory, are showing divisions over the president's war in Iran and immigration policies, according to a new Reuters/Ipsos poll.

headlines.headline: US Deports Iranian Pro-Democracy Activist to Central African Republic
headlines.summary: The United States deported an Iranian pro-democracy activist to Central African Republic, her lawyer said, describing it as a "super dangerous" transfer to a country with which the activist has no connection.

perspectives.angle: US Deports Iranian Pro-Democracy Activist to Central African Republic
perspectives.summary: The United States has deported an Iranian pro-democracy activist to the Central African Republic, her lawyer said, describing it as a "super dangerous" transfer to a country with which the activist has no connection.

headlines.headline: Azerbaijan Sentences Prominent Rights Activist to Eight Years in Prison
headlines.summary: A court in Baku convicted prominent rights activist of hooliganism and fraud in a case his lawyer called politically motivated. The sentencing raises concerns about human rights in the oil-rich Caucasus nation.

headlines.headline: US and Iran Signal Peace Deal Close as Military Action Continues Near Strait of Hormuz
headlines.summary: Washington and Tehran indicated they are near an agreement to end their three-month conflict, though Iranian drones targeting commercial ships were downed in the Strait of Hormuz. A senior US official said both sides agreed on a text and expect to sign an initial deal in coming days.

perspectives.angle: US and Iran Reach Interim Peace Deal to End War and Open Strait of Hormuz
perspectives.summary: The United States and Iran reached an initial agreement to open the Strait of Hormuz and extend a ceasefire in the Iran war, with signing scheduled for Friday in Switzerland. Pakistan mediated the deal, which gives 60 days to resolve issues around Iran's nuclear program.

perspectives.angle: Iran ceasefire limbo leaves world between peace and war
perspectives.summary: Two months after a nominal cease-fire, Iran, Israel and the US remain in a dangerous stalemate with low-intensity clashes continuing. The New York Times examines the precarious state of negotiations and regional tensions.

perspectives.angle: Trump and Iran say agreement to end war is close
perspectives.summary: President Trump said the US may attend a signing ceremony within days, while Iranian officials denied any final agreement had been reached. A senior US official said both sides agreed on a text.

perspectives.angle: US and Iran Haggle Over Frozen Funds as Interim Deal Nears
perspectives.summary: Iran and the United States are discussing how to release frozen Iranian funds as both sides work toward an interim deal to end hostilities. Despite ongoing military strikes from both sides, diplomatic efforts have intensified.

headlines.headline: Trump says US military strike killed leader of Venezuela's Tren de Aragua gang
headlines.summary: President Trump announced US forces carried out a strike that killed Hector Rusthenford Guerrero Flores, leader of the Venezuelan prison gang Tren de Aragua, with Venezuela calling it a joint operation.

perspectives.angle: US strike kills Tren de Aragua leader with Venezuela cooperation
perspectives.summary: President Trump announced a "swift and lethal" US military strike killed Hector Rusthenford Guerrero Flores, leader of the Tren de Aragua gang, with Venezuelan assistance. The operation marks unprecedented cooperation between Washington and Caracas.

headlines.headline: Iran Mines Nuclear Site Entrance to Prevent US Uranium Recovery
headlines.summary: Iran has deliberately collapsed tunnels and mined entrances to its bombed Isfahan nuclear complex to deter any US attempt to retrieve the uranium stockpile, according to Israeli sources.

headlines.headline: US Military Assets in Europe Face Significant Cuts Under Trump
headlines.summary: The Trump administration plans major reductions to US military presence in Europe, creating uncertainty for British defence planning as welfare spending is flagged to help fund shortfalls.

headlines.headline: Kaja Kallas Faces Growing Criticism as EU Foreign Policy Chief
headlines.summary: The EU's High Representative Kaja Kallas is under mounting pressure, with critics highlighting structural shortcomings and tensions in European foreign policy coordination.

headlines.headline: India and France Deepen Strategic Partnership in Defence
headlines.summary: India and France are strengthening their long-standing strategic partnership with a renewed 10-year defence pact, including deeper cooperation on undersea capabilities.

perspectives.angle: India and France to Deepen Undersea Defense Cooperation
perspectives.summary: New Delhi and Paris are expanding military ties, particularly in underwater combat capabilities, as both nations respond to growing security challenges in the Indo-Pacific region.

headlines.headline: PM Modi Departs for France and Slovakia, G7 Summit on Agenda
headlines.summary: Indian Prime Minister Modi begins a two-nation European visit with bilateral talks in France and Slovakia, ahead of G7 Summit participation.

headlines.headline: Brazilian refineries sold fuel to firm linked to terrorist organization
headlines.summary: Reuters reports Brazilian refineries sold over 100 million liters of naphtha to a firm under investigation for fraud involving a U.S.-designated terrorist group, raising concerns about supply chain integrity.

perspectives.angle: Brazil refineries linked to terrorist financing scheme
perspectives.summary: Brazilian refineries sold over 100 million liters of naphtha to a firm under investigation for fraud involving a criminal gang designated by the US as a terrorist organization, Reuters reports.

headlines.headline: Kennedy Center misses court deadline to remove Trump name
headlines.summary: The Kennedy Center asked for more time to remove President Trumps name from its facade after missing a Friday deadline, citing thunderstorms. Officials say removal expected by Saturday noon.

headlines.headline: Over 225,000 Russian soldiers identified killed in Ukraine
headlines.summary: Russian independent media Mediazona, working with BBC Russian service, confirmed identities of over 225,000 Russian soldiers killed in Ukraine, marking one of the most comprehensive casualty tallies.

perspectives.angle: More Than 200 18-Year-Old Russian Soldiers Killed in Ukraine War, New Data Shows
perspectives.summary: A new investigation identifies over 226,000 Russian war dead in Ukraine, including at least 200 18-year-old soldiers, highlighting the devastating human cost of the conflict on Russia's youngest conscripts.

headlines.headline: Elon Musk becomes worlds first trillionaire after SpaceX IPO
headlines.summary: BBC reports Elon Musk became the first trillionaire as SpaceX shares surged in their Nasdaq debut, valuing the company at over $2 trillion and making it the sixth-largest U.S. company.

headlines.headline: Pakistan Confirms Final Text of US-Iran Peace Deal Reached
headlines.summary: Pakistani Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif announced that a final, agreed-upon text for a peace deal between the United States and Iran has been reached, with both sides working on implementation steps.

headlines.headline: China Arrests US Scholar on Suspicion of Spying
headlines.summary: China confirmed the arrest of Min Zin, a US citizen who leads a think-tank focused on Myanmar, on suspicion of espionage and endangering Chinese national security.

perspectives.angle: China arrests US scholar of Myanmar on spying suspicion
perspectives.summary: China confirmed the arrest of Min Zin, a US citizen who leads a think-tank focused on Myanmar, on suspicion of spying and endangering national security. The detention raises tensions between Washington and Beijing.

headlines.headline: EU Greenlights First Phase of Membership Talks for Ukraine and Moldova
headlines.summary: Ambassadors from all 27 EU countries agreed to advance membership negotiations with Ukraine and Moldova, with the first phase of accession talks set to begin on Monday.

perspectives.angle: EU advances membership talks with Ukraine and Moldova
perspectives.summary: EU ambassadors agreed to begin the first phase of membership negotiations with Ukraine and Moldova, a significant step toward potential EU expansion.

headlines.headline: Philippines Death Toll Rises to 61 After Powerful Mindanao Earthquake
headlines.summary: The death toll from the magnitude 7.8 earthquake that struck the southern Philippines has climbed to 61, with disaster agencies continuing rescue and recovery operations across Mindanao.

perspectives.angle: Over 500,000 Affected by Powerful M7.8 Earthquake in Philippines
perspectives.summary: A magnitude 7.8 earthquake struck off Mindanao, affecting more than half a million people, though no tsunami warning was issued. Cleanup efforts are underway.

perspectives.angle: Philippines earthquake kills at least 55 on Mindanao island
perspectives.summary: A powerful earthquake struck Mindanao in the Philippines, killing at least 55 people and leaving a trail of destruction as the country picks up the pieces from its strongest quake in decades.

headlines.headline: Antarctica Records Winter Temperatures 20C Above Normal
headlines.summary: Scientists are observing a "huge anomaly" as Antarctica experiences a prolonged heatwave with temperatures 20 degrees Celsius warmer than normal for winter, raising concerns about accelerating ice loss.

perspectives.angle: Antarctica missing sea ice area size of France as temperatures soar 20C above average
perspectives.summary: A vast area of the Bellingshausen Sea on Antarctica west coast should be covered by sea ice but is missing, with experts calling the loss unprecedented and temperatures peaking 20 degrees above average.

headlines.headline: Ukraine to request $20 billion from allies to maintain battlefield momentum
headlines.summary: Ukraine is seeking additional military funding from allies to cement its battlefield advantage over Russia, as Kyiv looks to sustain gains in the four-year-old war.

perspectives.angle: Ukraine requests $20 billion from allies to sustain momentum
perspectives.summary: Ukraine is seeking an additional $20 billion in military funding from allies to cement its battlefield advantage over Russia. The request comes as Kyiv confirms strikes on Russian refineries and Putin announces satellite-based drone control systems.

headlines.headline: AI executives from Anthropic, OpenAI, Google to attend G7 summit
headlines.summary: Tech executives will join G7 leaders in France next week to discuss artificial intelligence governance, online safety, and economic challenges.

headlines.headline: Anthropic suspends new AI models after US security concerns
headlines.summary: Anthropic has suspended its powerful new AI models Fable 5 and Mythos 5 after US authorities raised security concerns just days following their public release. The company said the government identified a method to jailbreak the systems.

headlines.headline: G7 Summit Offers Trump and Modi Chance to Reset Ties After US Strikes Kill Indian Sailors
headlines.summary: The upcoming G7 summit provides an opportunity for Trump and Modi to mend bilateral relations after US military strikes in Iran accidentally killed Indian sailors, with both leaders seeking to contain tensions.

headlines.headline: Ukraine Plans to Recruit Foreign Nationals for Up to Half of Frontline Assault Troops
headlines.summary: Ukraine is preparing to open military recruitment to more foreign nationals, aiming for foreigners to fill 30-50% of assault and storm brigade positions amid severe manpower shortages.

headlines.headline: Inside Rubicon: The Structure of Russia's Elite Drone Center
headlines.summary: Analysis of Russia's newly established Rubicon Center for drone warfare, one of the Russian military's most important developments in 2024-2025, reveals how Moscow is organizing its advanced drone capabilities.

headlines.headline: UAE agrees to unlock billions of dollars for Iran, sources say
headlines.summary: The United Arab Emirates has agreed to unlock between  billion and  billion for Iran, sources told Reuters, in a tactical shift after weeks of Iranian attacks on the Gulf state during the US-Israeli war with Iran.

perspectives.angle: UAE to unlock billions in frozen Iranian funds amid ceasefire push
perspectives.summary: The United Arab Emirates has agreed to unlock billions of dollars for Iran, sources say, in a tactical shift after weeks of Iranian attacks on the Gulf state during the US-Israel war with Iran.

perspectives.angle: UAE to unlock frozen Iranian funds amid US ceasefire push
perspectives.summary: The United Arab Emirates agreed to unlock billions of dollars for Iran in a tactical shift after weeks of Iranian pressure. The move comes as US-Iran peace negotiations appear close to agreement.

headlines.headline: European shares rally as Middle East peace hopes push oil lower
headlines.summary: European stocks surged more than 1% as renewed hopes for a diplomatic breakthrough in the Middle East dragged oil prices lower and injected fresh optimism into markets.

headlines.headline: SpaceX $2 trillion debut divides markets amid cheers and protests
headlines.summary: Elon Musk's SpaceX soared 11% on its first day of trading, reaching a $2 trillion valuation. The record IPO drew both celebration and criticism from investors concerned about valuation and Musk's influence.

perspectives.angle: SpaceX soars 28% in record  trillion IPO debut
perspectives.summary: Elon Musk's SpaceX made history with the largest IPO ever, raising $75 billion and reaching a $2.25 trillion valuation. Musk becomes the world's first trillionaire as shares opened at $135 and surged on Nasdaq.

headlines.headline: Hong Kong Tests Heavy-Duty Drones for Construction in Low-Altitude Economy Push
headlines.summary: Hong Kong is testing drones capable of carrying 150kg loads of cement and bricks as part of the government's initiative to develop policies for a low-altitude economy and modernize construction logistics.

headlines.headline: China's World's Largest Smoking Population Raises Economic Concerns
headlines.summary: Public health experts warn that China's low taxes on cigarettes risk undermining Beijing's pursuit of a healthier population and could have broader economic implications for the world's second-largest economy.

headlines.headline: Trump Asks Republicans to Formally Erase His Impeachments from Historical Record
headlines.summary: President Donald Trump is urging Republicans in Congress to pass a resolution that would symbolically erase his two impeachments from the congressional record, marking an unprecedented effort to rewrite legislative history.

headlines.headline: Putin Announces Satellite-Based Drone Control System for Russian Military
headlines.summary: President Vladimir Putin revealed Russia is developing satellite-based control systems for combat drones, Interfax reported. The announcement comes amid ongoing hostilities with Ukraine and signals continued military technology investment.

headlines.headline: China Confirms Arrest of US Scholar Min Zin on Espionage Charges
headlines.summary: China's foreign ministry confirmed the arrest of Min Zin, a US citizen who leads a Myanmar-focused think tank, on suspicion of spying and endangering national security. The arrest adds to US-China tensions.

perspectives.angle: China Confirms Arrest of US Citizen Suspected of Spying
perspectives.summary: China has confirmed the arrest of a US citizen suspected of espionage activities. The arrest adds to growing tensions between Beijing and Washington amid ongoing disputes over trade, technology, and regional security.

headlines.headline: SpaceX Surges Past $2 Trillion Valuation in Record Nasdaq Debut
headlines.summary: Elon Musk's SpaceX saw shares jump 19% in its Nasdaq debut, pushing the company's value past $2 trillion and making Musk the world's first trillionaire. The IPO raised a record $75 billion.

perspectives.angle: SpaceX Makes Historic Stock Market Debut After Record IPO
perspectives.summary: SpaceX began trading on Nasdaq after the largest initial public offering in history, with shares opening at $150 and Elon Musk becoming the worlds first trillionaire as investors backed his vision spanning rockets and orbital AI.

headlines.headline: North Korea condemns US missile sale approval to South Korea
headlines.summary: North Korea's foreign ministry condemned a US decision to approve the sale of advanced air-to-air missiles to South Korea, warning it would worsen tensions on the Korean peninsula.

perspectives.angle: South Korea ex-President Yoon sentenced to 30 years over North Korea drone plot
perspectives.summary: A Seoul court sentenced former President Yoon Suk Yeol to 30 years in prison for ordering military drones to infiltrate North Korea, intended to create a pretext for his failed December 2024 martial law declaration.

headlines.headline: Pope Leo XIV flight grounded in Spain, king offers help
headlines.summary: Pope Leo XIV faced an unexpected travel delay when his charter flight back to Rome was grounded due to a technical issue. Spain's King Felipe offered assistance.

headlines.headline: US and Iran Near Deal to Reopen Strait of Hormuz
headlines.summary: A US-Iran agreement to reopen the strategic Strait of Hormuz could be signed during next week's G7 summit, with both sides expected to extend their ceasefire by approximately two months.

headlines.headline: UAE Denies $3 Billion Transfer to Iran
headlines.summary: The UAE categorically denied reports that it had transferred $3 billion to Iran as part of a deal to end attacks on the Gulf country, amid ongoing diplomatic negotiations.

headlines.headline: Explosion Reported in Southern Iranian Port City of Sirik
headlines.summary: Explosions were reported late Friday in the area of Sirik port in southern Iran, according to Iranian state television, in a region near the strategic Strait of Hormuz.

headlines.headline: UK Sets 2027 Deadline for Ban on Russian-Derived Fuel
headlines.summary: The UK announced it will fully phase in its ban on diesel and jet fuel produced from Russian crude oil by 2027, tightening sanctions on Moscow's energy exports.

headlines.headline: Paramount-Warner merger cleared by DOJ antitrust review
headlines.summary: The US Justice Department determined that Paramount Skydance's proposed acquisition of Warner Bros. Discovery is unlikely to harm competition or consumers, clearing a major regulatory hurdle for the media megamerger.

headlines.headline: Pope Leo warns Europe's migrant traffickers to repent or face hell
headlines.summary: Pope Leo issued a stern warning to human traffickers exploiting desperate migrants trying to reach Europe through Spain's Canary Islands, telling them to repent or face divine judgment.

perspectives.angle: Pope Leo warns migrant traffickers to repent or face divine judgment
perspectives.summary: During his Spain visit, the Pope issued a sharp condemnation of human traffickers exploiting migrants in the Canary Islands route, calling for repentance while urging host countries to uphold humane migration policies.

headlines.headline: Iran's Stranglehold on Strait of Hormuz Loosens as Gulf Oil Reaches Market
headlines.summary: Iran's ability to block the critical Strait of Hormuz appears to be weakening as Gulf Arab states find alternative routes for their oil exports. The development could reshape global energy markets and reduce Iran's leverage in negotiations.

headlines.headline: Global submarine arms race intensifies as 480+ subs from 40+ nations patrol world oceans
headlines.summary: More than 480 submarines from over 40 nations now operate globally, with regional conflicts in the Indo-Pacific, Middle East, and South Asia driving demand. The US Navy sank an Iranian corvette with a torpedo in March, while South Korea markets its submarines to Canada in an 8,400-mile demonstration cruise.

headlines.headline: Documentary tracks Wagner Group rise and the global mercenary threat to democracy
headlines.summary: A new film "Hells Army" examines the Russian Wagner Groups operations in Ukraine, Syria, and Africa, revealing how mercenary armies enable authoritarian regimes to seize resources while avoiding accountability. The documentary highlights how Wagner employed up to 50,000 fighters worldwide.

headlines.headline: Astronomers warn SpaceX orbital data centers will interfere with telescope observations
headlines.summary: SpaceX plans to launch AI-powered orbital data center satellites starting in 2027, with 70-meter spacecraft generating 150 kW of power. Astronomers warn the large, bright satellites will severely impact optical and radio astronomy, building on existing Starlink interference concerns.

headlines.headline: US surveillance authority set to expire as Iran conflict continues, threatening intelligence gaps
headlines.summary: Section 702 of FISA, responsible for 60% of President Daily Brief intelligence, will lapse at midnight Friday. With US forces potentially engaging Iran and threats from Russia, China, and terrorist groups active, critics warn the timing could not be worse for national security.

headlines.headline: UK economy contracts 0.1% in April as Iran war hits entertainment sector
headlines.summary: Britain's economy shrank for the first time since August, with the Iran war's cancellation of Formula 1 races and Gulf sporting events delivering a significant blow to the UK entertainment industry.

headlines.headline: US pledges  million to combat Ebola outbreak in Africa
headlines.summary: The US State Department announced $50 million in funding for the Coalition for Epidemic Preparedness Innovations to develop medical countermeasures for the rare Ebola strain spreading in the Democratic Republic of Congo.

headlines.headline: Russia builds major troop infrastructure along NATO's Arctic flank
headlines.summary: Russia is constructing facilities for tens of thousands of troops near NATO's northern border, prompting the alliance to bolster its Arctic defenses with new combat capabilities.

headlines.headline: Violence erupts at DRC rally opposing presidential term-limit change
headlines.summary: Security forces clashed with demonstrators in Kinshasa protesting proposed constitutional amendments that could extend President Tshisekedi's rule, amid rising tensions over democratic backsliding.

headlines.headline: ICE detains wife of US military veteran in immigration crackdown
headlines.summary: The arrest highlights the expanding scope of immigration enforcement under current policy, drawing criticism from advocates who note the spouse's family ties to US military service.

headlines.headline: Ukraine launches sweeping military service reforms with record pay raises to ease manpower crisis
headlines.summary: Ukraine announced landmark reforms to military service terms, including significant pay increases for infantry, as Kyiv struggles to address acute manpower shortages. The move comes as Russian forces continue pressure in eastern Ukraine.

headlines.headline: Myanmar factories reel from tight import controls as military prioritizes fuel
headlines.summary: Manufacturers in Myanmar are struggling with stricter import restrictions as the military government prioritizes fuel and essential goods. The controls highlight the economic strain from ongoing conflict and international sanctions.

headlines.headline: Pope Leo XIV concludes Spain visit amid Catholic revival hopes
headlines.summary: The pontiff's trip to Spain has spurred expectations among Catholic leaders of a spiritual revival in an increasingly secular nation.

perspectives.angle: Pope Leo XIV's flight home from Spain grounded by technical issue
perspectives.summary: Pope Leo XIV's Iberia charter flight was grounded by a technical problem after a weeklong visit to Spain, with the Spanish king coming to his aid.

headlines.headline: Ukraine and Russia trade drone strikes on energy infrastructure
headlines.summary: Both sides exchanged overnight drone attacks, with Ukraine striking oil refineries and a petrochemicals plant while Russia targeted railway stations and electrical substations.

perspectives.angle: Ukraine strikes Russian rubber plant and oil refineries in overnight drone attacks
perspectives.summary: Ukrainian forces conducted drone strikes on a Russian rubber plant and oil refineries overnight, continuing Kyiv's campaign against Russian infrastructure. The attacks come amid warnings of potential Russian missile launches from the Oreshnik site.

perspectives.angle: Ukraine and Russia Exchange Overnight Drone Strikes, Targeting Energy and Rail Systems
perspectives.summary: Russia and Ukraine carried out far-reaching drone strikes overnight, with Ukraine hitting a major oil processing region while Russia targeted railway stations and electrical substations.

headlines.headline: Latin American markets rally on US-Iran peace deal prospects
headlines.summary: Latin American assets rose as markets welcomed progress toward an agreement ending the US-Iran conflict, boosting global risk appetite.

perspectives.angle: UAE Stock Markets Hit Two-Month High on Iran Deal Optimism
perspectives.summary: Stock markets in the United Arab Emirates surged Friday as regional hopes grew for a potential ceasefire deal between Iran and the United States that could end months of conflict in the Gulf.

headlines.headline: Israeli-Palestinian Civil Society Groups Appeal for Two-State Solution in France
headlines.summary: Israeli and Palestinian civil society groups delivered an appeal in France urging the international community not to abandon a two-state solution as Paris seeks to keep the issue alive amid regional conflict.

perspectives.angle: Israel-Palestinian civil society groups urge international action in France as two-state solution dims
perspectives.summary: Israeli and Palestinian civil society organizations met in France to deliver an appeal urging the international community not to abandon the two-state solution amid diminishing prospects. The meeting highlights civil efforts to maintain dialogue outside official diplomatic channels.

headlines.headline: How the EU Can Respond to the Second China Shock
headlines.summary: European leaders face mounting pressure to address China massive trade surplus and industrial policies. Reuters analyzes three distinct policy challenges requiring different EU responses at next week summit.

headlines.headline: Anthropic Claude Fable 5 restrictions create new hurdles for China AI labs
headlines.summary: Access restrictions on Anthropic powerful model, reportedly targeted at Chinese AI labs, are sparking debate about safety versus innovation in the global AI race.

headlines.headline: Iran hawks circle as emerging US-Iran deal details draw scrutiny
headlines.summary: Iran hawks in Washington are already circling as details emerge about a potential agreement that Trump said could be signed as early as this weekend. Vice President Vance stated Iran will get no cash from any deal.

headlines.headline: UAE expedites South Korean Cheongung II missile defense delivery
headlines.summary: The UAE sent C-17 transport aircraft to South Korea to expedite delivery of the third M-SAM II (Cheongung II) battery, highlighting Gulf states defensive preparations amid regional tensions.

perspectives.angle: UAE Sends Military Planes to South Korea for Missile Defense Transport
perspectives.summary: The United Arab Emirates dispatched military aircraft to South Korea to expedite transport of Korean-made Cheongung missile defense systems, highlighting deepening defense cooperation.

headlines.headline: UK judge links Palestine Action convictions to terrorism connection
headlines.summary: A UK judge ruled that activists convicted over a raid on an Elbit Systems UK site will be sentenced on the basis of a terrorism connection in a landmark ruling with implications for protest-related cases.

headlines.headline: US plans major cut to fighter jets and warships for NATO operations in Europe
headlines.summary: The United States plans to significantly reduce the aircraft and warships it makes available for NATO operations in Europe, the New York Times reported, citing two senior European officials. The move comes as the Trump administration shifts focus away from European defense.

perspectives.angle: US Plans Major Cuts to Air and Naval Assets for NATO Operations in Europe
perspectives.summary: The United States plans to significantly reduce aircraft and warships available for NATO operations in Europe, according to senior European officials. The move raises questions about Washington commitment to the alliance.

headlines.headline: Palestine Action activists to be sentenced as terrorists over UK raid on Israel defense firm
headlines.summary: Four activists convicted of criminal damage at an Elbit Systems factory near Bristol will be sentenced as terrorists in what is believed to be a legal first in the UK. The ruling means they will not qualify for early release provisions.

headlines.headline: Wall Street confronts newly led Federal Reserve as rate hike fears rattle markets
headlines.summary: A suddenly rocky U.S. stock market confronts a potential wildcard next week: A newly led Federal Reserve at a time investors are worried that interest rate hikes to fight inflation could dampen enthusiasm for equities.

headlines.headline: Ukraine Air Force Warns of High-Probability Russian Missile Launch
headlines.summary: Ukraine's Air Force warned of a high probability of a Russian missile launch from the Oreshnik site within the next day, expected to come from the Kapustin Yar launch site in southern Russia.

headlines.headline: China-Russia Border River Tests Beijing's Ties With North Korea
headlines.summary: A tiny river on Russia's border is testing China's relationship with Kim Jong-un, as Beijing seeks Sea of Japan access that neither China nor Pyongyang have publicly acknowledged.

headlines.headline: Ukraine's Defense AI Chief Predicts 'New Paradigm' of Warfare
headlines.summary: Ukraine's defense AI chief says the system that possesses more data and better understands it will gain advantage, predicting a fundamental shift in how warfare is conducted.

headlines.headline: Iraq Foils Plot to Assassinate National Security Service Chief
headlines.summary: Iraqi security forces foiled a plot to assassinate the National Security Service chief and senior officers, arresting suspects linked to a banned Baath Party front group.

headlines.headline: Iran Agrees to Performance-Based Deal Requiring Major Concessions
headlines.summary: Iran has agreed to a performance-based deal that requires Tehran to destroy its nuclear material and dismantle its nuclear program, with none of its money released until compliance is verified.

headlines.headline: Indonesia's $8 Million Visa Scandal Threatens Talent and Investor Drive
headlines.summary: A major visa scandal involving $8 million threatens Indonesia's drive to attract talent and investors, as public-sector corruption remains an 'open secret' in the country.

headlines.headline: Trump Picks Wall Street's 'Top Cop' as New US Spy Chief
headlines.summary: Trump selected Jay Clayton, former SEC chair known as Wall Street's 'top cop,' as the new US spy chief after facing backlash over his initial choice of Pulte.

headlines.headline: Pakistan Unveils $67.5B Budget as Economy Reaches Record $452B
headlines.summary: Pakistan unveiled an 18.77 trillion-rupee budget for fiscal year 2026-27 as its economy reached a record $452 billion, with proposals for higher development spending.

headlines.headline: Oil Prices Fall to Near Two-Month Lows as Trump Cancels Planned Strikes on Iran
headlines.summary: Crude dropped nearly 3% to its lowest level in nearly two months after President Trump called off new strikes on Iran, reducing fears of escalation following tit-for-tat attacks earlier in the week.

perspectives.angle: Oil Falls to Near Two-Month Lows as Trump Calls Off Iran Strikes
perspectives.summary: Crude prices dropped over 4% after President Trump cancelled new strikes on Iran, reducing fears of escalation. The move signals potential de-escalation in the US-Iran conflict that has disrupted oil shipments through the Strait of Hormuz.

headlines.headline: US Senate Seeks $750 Million for Ukraine in $1.15 Trillion Defense Bill
headlines.summary: The massive Senate defense bill supports $1.15 trillion for total defense spending and includes $750 million in assistance for Ukraine, determining everything from ships and aircraft purchases to troop pay raises and addressing geopolitical threats.

headlines.headline: Iran, Israel and US Remain Locked in Low-Intensity Conflict Despite Nominal Cease-Fire
headlines.summary: Two months after announcing a cease-fire, all three sides remain in a dangerous limbo between peace and war, with ongoing low-intensity operations and unresolved tensions over the nuclear program.

headlines.headline: EU Leaders to Discuss Response to Second China Shock at Upcoming Summit
headlines.summary: Europe faces a new economic jolt from China driven by three distinct policies, with the $1.2 trillion trade surplus inspiring both envy and fear among EU leaders meeting next week to coordinate responses.

headlines.headline: Switzerland to Vote on Population Cap in Referendum with Europe-Wide Implications
headlines.summary: Swiss voters decide Sunday on capping population at 10 million in a referendum compared to Brexit for its potential economic and EU relations impact. The measure would require reducing immigration and could strain bilateral agreements with the EU.

perspectives.angle: Swiss Voters Reject Population Cap Referendum
perspectives.summary: Nearly 55% of Swiss voters rejected a proposal to cap population at 10 million by limiting migration. The right-wing initiative aimed to address housing and infrastructure concerns.

headlines.headline: Indonesian Students Protest Government Policies Amid Economic Strain
headlines.summary: Hundreds of students rallied in Jakarta against President Prabowo Subianto's spending priorities and fuel price hikes. Demonstrators criticized subsidies and free meals programs as the country faces growing economic pressures.

headlines.headline: US plans major cuts to NATO fighter jets and warships in Europe
headlines.summary: The United States plans to significantly reduce the aircraft and warships it makes available for NATO operations in Europe, the New York Times reported, citing two senior European officials.

perspectives.angle: NATO Weighs Alternative Defense Plans for Europe as US Shifts Focus
perspectives.summary: NATO"s top military officer is developing contingency plans to defend Europe against potential Russian aggression without heavy US reliance, amid concerns over American strategic pivot.

perspectives.angle: NATO Weighs Alternative Defense Plans as US Prepares for Conflicts Elsewhere
perspectives.summary: NATO's top military officer is developing contingency plans to defend Europe from Russian aggression without full US support, as Washington shifts focus to other global conflicts. The planning reflects growing uncertainty about America's commitment to European security.

headlines.headline: North Korea expresses full support for Russia in National Day message
headlines.summary: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un sent a congratulatory message to Russian President Vladimir Putin on Russia's National Day, voicing full support for Moscow's domestic and foreign policies.

perspectives.angle: North Korea signals full support for Russia on National Day
perspectives.summary: Kim Jong Un sent a congratulatory message to Putin emphasizing continued support for Russia, deepening the military partnership between the two nations.

headlines.headline: US-Iran interim agreement takes shape ahead of potential Sunday signing
headlines.summary: The US and Iran have reached a preliminary understanding with a memorandum that could be signed Sunday in Geneva. Iran insists any deal must also end fighting in Lebanon, while details are still being finalized.

headlines.headline: Analysis: Iran-US Prolonged Negotiations Increase Escalation Risk
headlines.summary: Israeli defense analysis warns that drawn-out negotiations between Iran and the US, combined with ongoing military exchanges, are heightening the risk of deliberate or accidental escalation.

headlines.headline: IDF Destroys Hamas Weapons Depots in Central Gaza Overnight
headlines.summary: Israeli military says it destroyed three Hamas weapons depots in central Gaza overnight, eliminating threats to troops operating in the area.

headlines.headline: Russian Supply Lines to Crimea Face Increasing Pressure
headlines.summary: Ukrainian operations are targeting the land corridor to Crimea, making Russia's occupation more vulnerable as supply routes come under sustained attack.

headlines.headline: Ukraines Drone Commander Plots Crimea Isolation Campaign
headlines.summary: Ukraines drone forces chief reveals plans to sever Crimeas connection to Russia through coordinated strikes on the Kerch Bridge and logistics routes. The strategy aims to isolate the occupied peninsula from Russian supply lines.

headlines.headline: Questions Mount Over US Claims of Secret Hormuz Oil Mission
headlines.summary: Trump claims hundreds of tankers have escaped Irans blockade through a secret US operation, but shipping data raises questions. The reported shipments appear to be increasing but the details remain murky.

headlines.headline: Nigeria Claims 13,000 Terrorists Neutralized in Past Year
headlines.summary: Nigerian president says the military has killed more than 13,000 terrorists in the last year as the country battles armed groups including Boko Haram. The announcement comes amid ongoing security challenges in the north.

headlines.headline: Europe Blaming China's Economic Rise for Its Own Failures, Think Tank Says
headlines.summary: A new analysis argues European policymakers are scapegoating China rather than addressing structural weaknesses in their own economies, amid tensions over trade and competitiveness.

headlines.headline: China Accuses Spy Turtles of Snooping on Its Coast
headlines.summary: Beijing claims marine animals fitted with surveillance equipment are being used to monitor Chinese waters, in an unusual accusation highlighting ongoing tensions over intelligence gathering.

headlines.headline: Iran-Linked Group Claims Hack of FBI Drones
headlines.summary: A group with alleged ties to Iran says it compromised US drone systems, raising concerns about cyber vulnerabilities in American security infrastructure.

headlines.headline: Oil Prices Slide as Traders Bet on Diplomacy
headlines.summary: Crude prices continued their decline as markets respond positively to signs of de-escalation between the US and Iran, with hopes for a diplomatic resolution rising.

headlines.headline: Israeli Firm BlackCore Meddled in US and Scottish Elections, French Watchdog Says
headlines.summary: A French regulatory body has accused an Israeli company of interfering in democratic processes in multiple countries, expanding concerns about election integrity.

headlines.headline: UK Defense Secretary Resigns, Warns Military Lacks Resources for Rising Threats
headlines.summary: John Healey resigned from Prime Minister Keir Starmer's government, accusing it of failing to adequately fund the military amid growing global threats. The resignation raises concerns about NATO burden-sharing as the US considers cutting its European presence.

perspectives.angle: UK Defense Secretary Resigns Over Inadequate Military Spending
perspectives.summary: British Defense Secretary John Healey resigned, telling Prime Minister Keir Starmer that the government's Defense Investment Plan falls short of what is required at a dangerous time. The resignation exposes spending challenges as NATO summit looms.

headlines.headline: Tech Executives from OpenAI, Anthropic, Google to Attend G7 Summit
headlines.summary: AI leaders including Sam Altman, Dario Amodei, and Demis Hassabis will join G7 leaders in France next week to discuss AI governance and online safety. The meeting comes as two of the companies prepare for landmark IPOs.

headlines.headline: Geneva Steps Up Security for 50,000-Strong Anti-G7 Protest
headlines.summary: Swiss authorities are boarding up businesses and deploying security ahead of a major anti-G7 protest expected this weekend. France hosts the summit next week in neighboring Évian-les-Bains.

perspectives.angle: Geneva Steps Up Security for 50,000-Strong G7 Protest
perspectives.summary: Swiss authorities are preparing for a massive anti-G7 protest expected this weekend in Geneva as France hosts the Group of Seven summit. Shopkeepers have boarded up businesses ahead of the demonstration.

headlines.headline: China cancels high-level diplomatic meetings with EU at short notice
headlines.summary: Beijing abruptly cancelled two diplomatic meetings with the European Union planned for June, Financial Times reports. The move signals growing tensions between China and Europe amid trade disputes and geopolitical friction.

perspectives.angle: China Cancels High-Level Diplomatic Meetings With EU in Beijing
perspectives.summary: China cancelled at short notice two diplomatic meetings planned with the European Union in Beijing this month, the Financial Times reported, raising questions about the state of EU-China relations.

headlines.headline: G7 summit to spotlight global economic imbalances as China exports surge
headlines.summary: France uses its G7 presidency to highlight mounting global trade tensions driven by China export surge, chronic US deficits, and weak European investment. The summit in Evian comes as world economy faces financial shock risks.

headlines.headline: German Army Chief Warns Russia Could Attack NATO by 2029
headlines.summary: German army chief Lt. Gen. Carsten Brecht warned that Russia will be prepared to attack NATO by 2029, citing "NATO-agreed intelligence." The warning adds to growing Western concerns about Russian military buildup near alliance borders.

perspectives.angle: German Army Chief Warns Russia Will Be Ready to Attack NATO by 2029
perspectives.summary: German army chief Christian Freuding stated Russia will be prepared to attack NATO by 2029, calling it "NATO-agreed intelligence" and the latest in a series of warnings about Russian threat and Europe lack of preparedness.

headlines.headline: Israel Releases Hamas Co-Founder Hassan Yousef After Two Years Detention
headlines.summary: Israel has released Hamas co-founder Hassan Yousef after more than two years of detention. Yousef is a senior leader of Hamas in the West Bank, having co-founded the group in 1987 along with Sheikh Ahmed Yassin.

headlines.headline: Ebola Outbreak Spreads in Congo as Cracked Coffin Traced to Super-Spreader Event
headlines.summary: A pastor's coffin that broke on the rocky road to his burial is suspected as one of the earliest super-spreader events in Congo's growing Ebola epidemic.

perspectives.angle: Two Babies Die of Ebola in Congo Orphanage as Outbreak Spreads
perspectives.summary: Two infants have died of Ebola in a Congo orphanage, highlighting the particular risk children face in the ongoing outbreak. WHO reports testing has been stalled in three labs due to supply shortages.

headlines.headline: US and EU near deadline on $11.5 billion tariffs in decades-long aircraft dispute
headlines.summary: Washington and Brussels have yet to decide whether to continue suspending or reimpose tariffs on $11.5 billion of goods in a dispute over aircraft subsidies, with their truce expiring within days.

headlines.headline: Philippines Defense Chief Vows to Press on Despite Chinese Sanctions
headlines.summary: Philippine Defense Secretary Gilberto Teodoro vowed to continue his duties despite China sanctioning him for "erroneous remarks," calling out China's "wickedness" as tensions escalate over South China Sea disputes.

perspectives.angle: Philippines protests China sanctions against defense chief as unfriendly act
perspectives.summary: Manila condemned Beijing sanctions on Defense Secretary Gilberto Teodoro, calling them an "unfriendly act." China sanctioned Teodoro and his family for "erroneous remarks" about China, further escalating tensions between the two nations.

headlines.headline: UK Defense Minister Resigns Over Defense Funding
headlines.summary: UK Armed Forces Minister Al Carns resigned in protest over lack of defense funding, following Defense Secretary John Healey's earlier resignation. Prime Minister Keir Starmer faces a political crisis as two defense ministers quit within days.

headlines.headline: ECB signals option to raise rates again in July after insurance hike
headlines.summary: The European Central Bank raised interest rates for the first time in nearly three years, with Bundesbank President Nagel saying all options remain open for July to combat Iran-war-driven energy inflation.

headlines.headline: UK lags European rivals in curbing influx of cheap parcels from China
headlines.summary: More cheap parcels are flowing into the UK from China than into rival European economies, undermining British retailers and raising concerns about the UKs ability to address the trade imbalance.

headlines.headline: World chess body suspends Russia after sports tribunal ruling
headlines.summary: The International Chess Federation suspended Russia following a Court of Arbitration for Sport ruling in favor of Ukraine, ending decades of Russian dominance in the game.

headlines.headline: Disaster drills helped prevent more deaths in powerful Philippine earthquake
headlines.summary: Philippine officials say years of disaster-preparedness drills helped prevent a larger casualty toll when a powerful magnitude 7.8 earthquake struck the southern Philippines.

headlines.headline: Fuel Stations in Crimea Run Dry After Ukrainian Drone Strikes
headlines.summary: Fuel stations on the Russian-held Crimean peninsula were out of petrol on Thursday as a Ukrainian campaign against supply lines escalates, highlighting the effectiveness of Ukrainian strikes on Russian logistics.

perspectives.angle: Ukraine Strikes Fuel Supplies in Crimea, Creating Shortages on Russian-Held Peninsula
perspectives.summary: Ukrainian forces targeted fuel supplies to Crimea, causing a fuel crisis on the occupied peninsula in a new blow to Moscow's war narrative.

headlines.headline: British Defence Minister John Healey Resigns Over Spending Dispute
headlines.summary: UK Defence Minister John Healey quit on Thursday, accusing Prime Minister Keir Starmer of failing to commit the resources needed to keep Britain safe from mounting threats. The resignation highlights tensions over military spending.

perspectives.angle: UK Defence Minister Resigns Over Military Spending Dispute
perspectives.summary: British Defence Minister John Healey quit over a months-long dispute with Prime Minister Keir Starmer over military spending, accusing him of failing to commit resources needed to keep Britain safe from mounting threats.

headlines.headline: Japan-South Korea Summit Aims to Deepen Cooperation Amid Regional Tensions
headlines.summary: Japanese PM Sanae Takaichi and South Korean President Lee Jae Myung held a summit on May 19, discussing enhanced cooperation in an increasingly volatile geopolitical environment.

headlines.headline: France, UK, Germany Envoys Meet Russian Foreign Ministry Officials
headlines.summary: Ambassadors from France, the UK, and Germany arrived at the Russian Foreign Ministry for meetings with senior diplomats, amid heightened tensions over the Ukraine war.

headlines.headline: Germany and France Scrap FCAS Fighter Jet Project
headlines.summary: In a major blow to European defense cooperation, Germany and France have abandoned their ambitious FCAS fighter jet project after years of planning and billions in investment.

headlines.headline: Trump cancels planned Iran strikes, says deal "approved" after key points agreed
headlines.summary: US President Donald Trump called off further strikes on Iran and announced that key points of a peace deal have been agreed, with a formal agreement expected within days.

perspectives.angle: Netanyahu Faces Bitter Pill as Trump's Iran Deal Nears
perspectives.summary: Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu, who had hoped the Iran war could spur regime change in Tehran, now faces a US-Iran deal that could complicate Israel's strategic position.

headlines.headline: Trump says "great" Iran settlement will reopen Strait of Hormuz within days
headlines.summary: President Trump announced a "great settlement" with Iran could be signed within days, which would reopen the Strait of Hormuz and end hostilities. Oil prices extended losses on the news.

perspectives.angle: Iran Closes Strait of Hormuz, Attacks US Bases in Kuwait and Bahrain
perspectives.summary: Iran announced the closure of the Strait of Hormuz to all vessels and launched retaliatory strikes on US military bases in Kuwait, Bahrain, and Jordan following American attacks. The escalation marks day 104 of the conflict as tit-for-tat strikes continue.

headlines.headline: Ukraine War Has Lasted Longer Than World War I
headlines.summary: The war in Ukraine has now exceeded the duration of World War I, with parallels emerging between the grinding nature of both conflicts and the way new technologies have reshaped warfare. The milestone underscores the protracted nature of the conflict.

perspectives.angle: Ukraine War Duration Exceeds World War I
perspectives.summary: The conflict in Ukraine has now lasted longer than World War I, marking a grim milestone as fighting continues with no clear end in sight. The war has entered its fourth year with ongoing casualties and infrastructure destruction.

headlines.headline: China Sanctions Philippine Defense Secretary Over South China Sea Remarks
headlines.summary: China's foreign ministry announced sanctions against Philippine Defense Secretary Gilberto Teodoro and his close relatives, accusing him of making "erroneous remarks" that undermined China's legitimate interests and bilateral ties.
